editorial.title: Real test behind market surge
editorial.body: For decades, Korea’s stock market was synonymous with untapped potential. Home to some of the world’s most competitive manufacturers and tech firms...
editorial.pullQuote: Korea's stock market was synonymous with untapped potential for decades.
summary: Korea's stock market has long represented untapped potential for global investors.
The nation hosts world-class manufacturers and tech firms driving economic growth.
Recent market surge tests whether Korea can finally deliver on its competitive promise.
editorial.stance: Editorial questions whether Korea's market surge reflects genuine transformation or cyclical hype.
summaryTr: 한국 증시는 오랫동안 글로벌 투자자들에게 미개발 잠재력으로 평가받아왔다.
한국은 경제 성장을 주도하는 세계 수준의 제조업체와 기술 기업들을 보유하고 있다.
최근 시장 급등은 한국이 마침내 경쟁력 있는 공약을 실현할 수 있을지 시험하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr: 수십 년 동안 한국의 주식시장은 미개발된 잠재력의 대명사였다. 세계에서 가장 경쟁력 있는 제조업체와 기술 기업들의 본거지이면서도...

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: Editorial adopts a balanced analytical stance, acknowledging Korea's competitive strengths while raising skeptical questions about market fundamentals without partisan ideology or clear left/right framing.

editorial.title: 무산된 개헌, 극한의 정치 대립 결과다
editorial.body: (사설) 개헌안이 국회 표결에서 국민의힘의 불참으로 무산됐다. 1987년 헌법의 경직성 완화와 대통령 계엄 권한 제한 등이 핵심이었으나 여야 합의 부재와 정치 대립으로 개헌 논의가 좌절됐다. |
summary: 국민의힘의 불참으로 개헌안이 국회 표결에서 무산됐다.
1987년 헌법 경직성 완화와 대통령 계엄 권한 제한이 쟁점이었다.
여야 합의 부재와 극한의 정치 대립이 개헌을 좌절시켰다.
editorial.pullQuote: 개헌안이 국회 표결에서 국민의힘의 불참으로 무산됐다.
editorial.stance: 극한의 정치 대립으로 인한 개헌 무산을 비판하며 여야 합의의 필요성을 촉구한다.
editorial.titleTr: Failed Constitutional Reform Reflects Deepening Political Divide
summaryTr: The constitutional amendment failed to reach a National Assembly vote due to the Democratic Party's absence.
The debate centered on easing the rigidity of the 1987 Constitution and limiting presidential emergency powers.
The absence of bipartisan agreement and extreme political confrontation derailed the constitutional revision.
editorial.bodyTr: The constitutional amendment bill failed to advance due to the People Power Party's absence from the National Assembly vote. While the core issues included easing the rigidity of the 1987 Constitution and limiting presidential martial law powers, the constitutional revision discussions collapsed due to the absence of bipartisan consensus and political confrontation.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 개헌안의 구체적 내용(계엄권 제한)을 지지도 비판도 하지 않고, 여야 양측의 '극한 대립'과 '합의 부재'를 동등하게 책임지우며 절충의 필요성만을 촉구하는 중립적 입장

editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Power's Violence Against Citizens: 31 People Indicted for Refusing Desired Testimony
summaryTr: The Democratic Party-led special investigation committee referred 31 witnesses for prosecution on charges of perjury.
It has sparked controversy as an exercise of state power against those who did not provide desired testimony.
Critics have raised concerns that this represents a violent investigation being perpetrated by those in power against the people.
editorial.bodyTr: The Special Committee on State Affairs Investigation referred 31 state inquiry witnesses to the Corruption Investigation Office and police on charges of perjury and other offenses on the 8th. Those referred by the Democratic Party are related to multiple cases involving President Lee Jae-myung that were allegedly fabricated through prosecutorial coercion, marking what amounts to state violence against the people orchestrated by those in power.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Don't Destroy the Constitution We Have Before Arguing for Constitutional Reform
summaryTr: Speaker Woo Won-sik halted the processing of a constitutional amendment bill that excluded the People Power Party.
Constitutional amendments require the approval of at least two-thirds of all members present.
Before destroying the current constitution, we must first protect the existing constitution.
editorials.bodyTr: Before advocating for constitutional revision, one must not destroy the existing constitution. National Assembly Speaker Woo Won-shuk halted the constitutional amendment bill he had been pushing forward while excluding the People Power Party on the 8th. editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Time to Shift Perception of Power Lines from Unwanted Facilities to Economic Lifeline for Regions
summaryTr: Expanding transmission lines is not a shunned facility but a lifeline for regional economies.
Building power grids in the AI era is a national priority.
Despite government-led efforts, expansion has stalled due to local resistance.
editorials.bodyTr: Power transmission lines are not facilities to be shunned, but rather the economic lifeline of communities—this is the mindset shift needed. Climate, Energy and Environment Minister Kim Sung-hwan met with representatives of local residents opposed to power grid construction. Expanding the power grid is an urgent national task in the AI era, and despite the government's efforts, including the enactment of special legislation, expansion efforts remain inadequate.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative

editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 한병도 더불어민주당 원내대표가 7일 오후 국회 본회의에서 개헌안 제안설명을 하고 있다. 국민의힘은 개헌 반대를 당론으로 정하고 본회의장에 들어오지 않았다. 임현동 기자 국회가 어제 본회의를 열고 여야 6개 정당이 발의한 개헌안에 대한 표결 처리에 나섰다. 하지만 당론으로 반대한 국민의힘 측이 불참하면서 표결 자체가 무산됐다. 여당은 표결을 재추진하겠다지만, 6·3 지방선거와 동시에 39년 만에 개헌 국민투표가 치러지기는 어려울 전망이다. 이번 개헌안은 헌법 전문에 부마민주항쟁과 5·18 민주화운동을 명시하고 계엄에 대한 국회의 통제권을 강화하는 내용을 담았다. 국민의힘도 개헌안 내용 자체에 대한 반대보다는 선거와 개헌 국민투표를 동시에 하는 데 대한 반대로 표결에 참여하지 않았다. 개헌안 발의에서부터 무산까지의 과정을 보면 여러 가지 아쉬운 대목이 있다. 현행 헌법 규정으로 보나, 정치적 견지에서 보나 제1 야당의 동의 없이 개헌안을 처리하는 것은 쉽지 않다. 그래서 여권이 초당적 협조를 이끌어내는 정치력을 발휘하느냐가 관건이었다. 우원식 국회의장은 가능한 것부터 하자며 순차적 부분 개헌을 들고 나왔지만, 발의 단계에서부터 국민의힘이 빠진 채 나머지 정당만으로 진행했다. 정말로 개헌에 의지와 진정성이 있다면, 발의 이전 단계부터 사회적 논의와 야당에 대한 설득을 거치는 게 옳았다. 개헌 국민투표가 선거에 어떤 영향을 끼치는지 밝히지도 않으면서 반대만 한 국민의힘도 비판받아 마땅하지만, 우 의장이 “개헌을 막는다면 12·3 계엄 반대의 진정성을 누가 믿겠느냐”고 압박한 것은 협조를 구하는 자세가 아니었다. “불법 계엄에 대한 국회의 통제를 강화하자는 데 반대하는 사람들은 불법계엄 옹호론자”라는 이재명 대통령의 국무회의 발언도 국민의힘의 반발만 불러 개헌안 통과를 더 어렵게 만들 뿐이었다. 대통령 직선제가 도입된 1987년 제9차 개헌은 국민투표에서 93.1%의 압도적인 지지를 받았었다. 국민적 공감대 위에서 당시 여야가 머리를 맞대 합의안을 만들었기 때문에 가능한 찬성률이었다. 당시 정치권은 여야 동수로 구성된 8인 정치회담을 꾸려 개헌 협상을 맡겼고, 한 달 만에 여야가 합의한 개헌안을 만들었다. 지금은 공소취소 특검법 등을 둘러싼 여야 대립이 심각하다. 개헌안을 재추진하려면 여권은 야당이 참여할 명분과 길부터 터주기 바란다. 국민의힘도 논의 자체를 거부하는 태도를 버려야 할 것이다. 다른 건 몰라도 개헌만큼은 여야 합의로 해야 한다.
editorial.pullQuote: 다른 건 몰라도 개헌만큼은 여야 합의로 해야 한다.
summary: 국회 본회의에서 여야 6개 정당이 발의한 개헌안 표결이 국민의힘 불참으로 무산됐다.
여권은 야당 설득 없이 일방적으로 진행했고, 야당도 논의 자체를 거부하는 태도를 보였다.
1987년 여야 합의로 93.1% 찬성을 얻은 사례처럼, 개헌은 반드시 여야 합의로 추진해야 한다.
editorial.stance: 개헌 추진을 위해서는 여권의 성의 있는 설득과 야당의 열린 자세가 모두 필요하다.
summaryTr: A constitutional amendment bill proposed by six ruling and opposition parties failed to be voted on at the National Assembly plenary session due to the ruling party's absence.
The ruling bloc proceeded unilaterally without persuading the opposition party, while the opposition also showed an attitude of refusing discussion itself.
Like the 1987 case where a ruling and opposition agreement resulted in 93.1% support, constitutional amendments must be pursued only through ruling and opposition consensus.
editorial.bodyTr: Parliament held a plenary session yesterday and proceeded to vote on constitutional amendment bills submitted by six ruling and opposition parties. However, the vote itself fell through as the People Power Party, which opposed the amendment as party policy, did not attend. The ruling party says it will push for another vote, but prospects are dim for holding a constitutional referendum alongside the June 3 local elections for the first time in 39 years. The current amendment bill includes provisions for the Busan-Masan Democratic Uprising and the May 18 Democratic Uprising in the constitutional preamble and strengthens parliamentary control over martial law. The People Power Party's decision not to participate in the vote stemmed not from opposition to the amendment's content itself, but from opposition to holding a referendum simultaneously with the elections. Looking at the process from the submission to the collapse of the amendment bill, there are several regrettable aspects. Both under current constitutional provisions and from a political standpoint, it is not easy to process a constitutional amendment without the consent of the main opposition party. Therefore, the key question was whether the ruling party could exercise its political influence to secure bipartisan cooperation. National Assembly Speaker Woo Won-shik proposed sequential partial amendments as a pragmatic approach, but proceeded from the submission stage onward with only the remaining parties while the People Power Party stayed out. If there had been genuine will and sincerity regarding constitutional reform, the proper course would have been to conduct social discussion and persuasion of the opposition party before the submission stage. While the People Power Party deserves criticism for merely opposing the referendum without clarifying how it would affect the elections, Speaker Woo's remark that "if we block constitutional reform, who would believe the sincerity of opposition to the December 3 martial law" was not the tone of someone seeking cooperation. President Lee Jae-myung's statement at the cabinet meeting that "those who oppose strengthening parliamentary control over illegal martial law are proponents of illegal martial law" only provoked backlash from the People Power Party and made passage of the amendment more difficult. The ninth constitutional amendment in 1987, when the direct presidential election system was introduced, received overwhelming support of 93.1% in a national referendum. This high approval rate was possible because the ruling and opposition parties reached a compromise agreement by putting their heads together on the basis of national consensus. At that time, the political circles established an eight-member political dialogue with equal representation from both parties to handle constitutional amendment negotiations, and within a month produced a constitutional amendment bill agreed upon by both sides. Currently, confrontation between the ruling and opposition parties over matters such as the special prosecutor law on case dismissals is severe. If the ruling party intends to push forward with the amendment again, it should first provide justification and a path for the opposition party to participate. The People Power Party, for its part, must abandon its attitude of refusing dialogue itself. Constitutional amendments above all else must be reached through ruling-opposition agreement.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 여야 양측을 균형있게 비판하며(여권의 압박 수사 및 이 대통령 발언 비판, 야당의 논의 거부 태도 비판) 합의의 중요성을 강조하는 중립적 입장을 유지

editorial.title: Time to weigh risks
editorial.body: Inflation, debt risks mount as Korea’s stock markets celebrate historic highs The number itself invites euphoria. Barely two months after crossing 6,000, South Korea’s benchmark index Kospi closed at 7,384.56 points on Wednesday, the first finish above the 7,000 level in the market’s history. On Thursday, the benchmark rose 1.43 percent to close at 7,490.05, after briefly topping 7,500. The advance pointed to still firm investor sentiment, but choppy trading also hinted at growing caution over the pace of this week’s rapid gains. The rally has not emerged from fantasy. Korea’s semiconductor boom has become one of the global economy’s defining stories this year. Samsung Electronics and SK hynix have surged on the back of the artificial intelligence infrastructure race, turning memory chips into strategic assets. Korea’s first quarter exports hit a record $219.9 billion, up 37.8 percent from a year earlier. Semiconductor shipments alone soared 139 percent to $78.5 billion as demand for AI servers intensified. Some investment banks now project the Kospi could reach 8,000 by year-end. There are deeper reasons behind the market’s rise. Long criticized for the “Korea discount,” Seoul has spent years trying to improve shareholder returns, corporate governance and capital market transparency. Those efforts appear to be feeding into valuations. Over the past year, the Kospi has risen as much as 188.5 percent, a pace rarely seen among advanced economies. Yet markets can become overly dependent on a single narrative. Korea’s current narrative rests heavily on chips, liquidity and confidence that the AI cycle will continue uninterrupted. Equally worrisome is that consumer prices rose 2.6 percent in April from a year earlier, the fastest increase in 21 months. Petroleum prices jumped 21.9 percent as the Middle East conflict disrupted energy markets and constrained shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The shock is spreading beyond fuel. Airfares, logistics costs and household expenses are all rising. Service prices are beginning to reflect higher transportation and insurance costs. Price caps and fuel tax cuts have softened the blow, but temporary buffers rarely solve structural pressures. The geopolitical dimension adds further uncertainty. US President Donald Trump this week claimed Iran had attacked a Korean cargo vessel and renewed pressure on Seoul to join maritime protection operations linked to the US-led mission in Hormuz. Whether those claims are fully verified or not, the episode underlines Korea’s vulnerability. Korea, with its economy heavily dependent on exports and imported energy, cannot isolate itself from distant conflicts. Oil shocks arrive quickly at Korean ports and even faster at Korean gas stations. The Bank of Korea has begun adjusting its tone. Yoo Sang-dai, the central bank’s senior deputy governor, recently suggested the time may have come not merely to pause rate cuts but to consider raising rates. That creates tension with the government’s expansionary fiscal instincts. President Lee Jae Myung has criticized what he calls fiscal conservatism and signaled support for active spending. Yet fiscal expansion becomes harder to sustain when inflation expectations are rising. Coordination between fiscal and monetary authorities will become increasingly important in the months ahead. The greater risk may lie in households rather than ministries. Margin lending in the stock market has climbed from 27 trillion won ($18.6 billion) at the end of last year to around 36 trillion won. In highly volatile markets, leverage often looks manageable until prices stop rising. The symbolism of the Kospi surpassing 7,000 points should not be dismissed. Korea’s capital markets have matured, and its chip industry now sits near the heart of the global AI economy. But stock indices are poor substitutes for economic balance. If the benefits of the export boom fail to spread beyond chipmakers and financial assets, the gap between market optimism and household reality will widen further. Markets, after all, can sprint ahead of economies for only so long before both rediscover the same altitude. summary: South Korea's Kospi hits 7,000 for first time, driven by semiconductor and AI boom.
Inflation surges 2.6% annually while margin lending doubles, signaling growing financial risks.
Geopolitical tensions and overreliance on chips threaten market gains unless benefits spread broadly.
editorial.stance: Celebrate Korea's market milestone cautiously—sustained growth requires tackling inflation, reducing leverage, and diversifying beyond semiconductors.
summaryTr: 한국의 코스피가 반도체와 AI 붐에 힘입어 처음으로 7,000을 돌파했다.
인플레이션이 연 2.6% 급증하고 마진 대출이 두 배로 증가하면서 금융 위험이 커지고 있다.
지정학적 긴장과 칩에 대한 과도한 의존도는 이익이 광범위하게 확산되지 않는 한 시장 상승폭을 위협하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
인플레이션, 부채 위험 증가 속에 한국 주식시장이 사상 최고점 경축

이 숫자 자체만으로도 희열을 불러일으킨다. 6000포인트를 돌파한 지 겨우 두 달여 만에 한국 벤치마크지수인 코스피가 수요일 7384.56포인트로 마감하며 역사상 처음으로 7000선을 넘었다. 목요일에는 벤치마크지수가 1.43% 오르며 7490.05로 마감했으며, 일시적으로 7500을 넘기도 했다. 이 같은 상승은 여전히 견고한 투자심리를 가리켰지만, 변동성 있는 거래는 이번 주 급등 속도에 대한 경계심이 커지고 있음을 시사했다.

이번 랠리는 허구에서 나온 것이 아니다. 한국의 반도체 호황은 올해 글로벌 경제의 정의적 이야기 중 하나가 되었다. 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스는 인공지능 인프라 경쟁에 힘입어 급등했으며, 메모리칩을 전략적 자산으로 만들었다. 한국의 1분기 수출액은 219억 9000만 달러로 1년 전 대비 37.8% 증가했다. 반도체 출하량만 139% 급증한 78억 5000만 달러를 기록했으며 AI 서버에 대한 수요가 심화되었다. 일부 투자은행은 코스피가 연말까지 8000에 도달할 수 있다고 전망하고 있다.

시장 상승의 배경에는 더 깊은 이유들이 있다. '코리아 디스카운트'로 오랫동안 비판받아온 서울은 주주수익, 기업지배구조, 자본시장 투명성 개선을 위해 수년간 노력해왔다. 이런 노력들이 밸류에이션에 반영되고 있는 것으로 보인다. 지난 1년간 코스피는 최대 188.5% 상승했으며, 이는 선진국 중에서도 거의 볼 수 없는 수준이다.

그러나 시장은 단일 담론에 과도하게 의존할 수 있다. 한국의 현재 담론은 반도체, 유동성, 그리고 AI 사이클이 계속될 것이라는 확신에 크게 의존하고 있다. 똑같이 우려스러운 것은 4월 소비자 물가가 전년 대비 2.6% 올랐다는 점이다. 이는 21개월 만에 가장 빠른 상승률이다. 석유 가격은 중동 분쟁이 에너지 시장을 교란시키고 호르무즈 해협을 통한 해운을 제약하면서 21.9% 급등했다. 이 충격은 연료를 넘어 확산되고 있다. 항공료, 물류비, 가계지출이 모두 상승하고 있다. 서비스 가격은 높아진 운송비와 보험료를 반영하기 시작했다. 물가 상한제와 유류세 인하가 타격을 완화했지만, 임시방편은 구조적 압력을 거의 해결하지 못한다.

지정학적 요소는 추가 불확실성을 더한다. 도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령은 이번 주 이란이 한국 화물선을 공격했다고 주장했으며 호르무즈 관련 미국 주도의 해상 보호 작전에 참여하도록 서울에 압력을 다시 가했다. 이 주장이 완전히 검증되든 아니든, 이 일화는 한국의 취약성을 보여준다. 수출과 수입 에너지에 크게 의존하는 경제를 가진 한국은 먼 분쟁으로부터 자신을 고립시킬 수 없다. 유가 충격은 한국 항구에 빠르게 도착하고 한국 주유소에서는 더욱 빠르게 나타난다.

한국은행이 기조를 조정하기 시작했다. 중앙은행의 유영상 부총재는 최근 단순히 인상을 멈출 때가 되었을 뿐만 아니라 인상을 고려할 시점이 되었을 수 있다고 시사했다. 이는 정부의 확장적 재정 본능과 긴장을 만든다. 이재명 대통령은 자신이 부르는 재정 보수주의를 비판했으며 적극적 지출 지원을 신호했다. 그러나 인플레이션 기대가 오를 때 재정 확장은 유지하기 어려워진다. 앞으로 몇 개월간 재정 및 통화 당국 간 조율이 점점 더 중요해질 것이다.

더 큰 위험은 부처가 아닌 가계에 있을 수 있다. 주식시장 신용거래금은 작년 말 27조 원에서 약 36조 원으로 증가했다. 변동성이 큰 시장에서 레버리지는 가격이 오를 때까지 관리 가능해 보인다. 코스피가 7000포인트를 돌파한 상징성을 무시할 수 없다. 한국의 자본시장은 성숙했으며 반도체 산업은 이제 글로벌 AI 경제의 중심부 근처에 있다. 그러나 주가지수는 경제 균형의 대체 수단이 되기에는 부족하다. 수출 호황의 혜택이 반도체 제조사와 금융자산 너머로 확산되지 않으면 시장 낙관론과 가계 현실 간 격차는 더욱 벌어질 것이다. 결국 시장은 두 지수가 같은 고도를 다시 발견하기 전까지만 경제를 앞지를 수 있을 뿐이다.

editorials.title: Time to weigh risks
editorials.body: Inflation, debt risks mount as Korea’s stock markets celebrate historic highs The number itself invites euphoria. Barely two months after crossing 6,000, South Korea’s benchmark index Kospi closed at 7,384.56 points on Wednesday, the first finish above the 7,000 level in the market’s history. On Thursday, the benchmark rose 1.43 percent to close at 7,490.05, after briefly topping 7,500. The advance pointed to still firm investor sentiment, but choppy trading also hinted at growing caution over the pace of this week’s rapid gains. The rally has not emerged from fantasy. Korea’s semiconductor boom has become one of the global economy’s defining stories this year. Samsung Electronics and SK hynix have surged on the back of the artificial intelligence infrastructure race, turning memory chips into strategic assets. Korea’s first quarter exports hit a record $219.9 billion, up 37.8 percent from a year earlier. Semiconductor shipments alone soared 139 percent to $78.5 billion as demand for AI servers intensified. Some investment banks now project the Kospi could reach 8,000 by year-end. There are deeper reasons behind the market’s rise. Long criticized for the “Korea discount,” Seoul has spent years trying to improve shareholder returns, corporate governance and capital market transparency. Those efforts appear to be feeding into valuations. Over the past year, the Kospi has risen as much as 188.5 percent, a pace rarely seen among advanced economies. Yet markets can become overly dependent on a single narrative. Korea’s current narrative rests heavily on chips, liquidity and confidence that the AI cycle will continue uninterrupted. Equally worrisome is that consumer prices rose 2.6 percent in April from a year earlier, the fastest increase in 21 months. Petroleum prices jumped 21.9 percent as the Middle East conflict disrupted energy markets and constrained shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The shock is spreading beyond fuel. Airfares, logistics costs and household expenses are all rising. Service prices are beginning to reflect higher transportation and insurance costs. Price caps and fuel tax cuts have softened the blow, but temporary buffers rarely solve structural pressures. The geopolitical dimension adds further uncertainty. US President Donald Trump this week claimed Iran had attacked a Korean cargo vessel and renewed pressure on Seoul to join maritime protection operations linked to the US-led mission in Hormuz. Whether those claims are fully verified or not, the episode underlines Korea’s vulnerability. Korea, with its economy heavily dependent on exports and imported energy, cannot isolate itself from distant conflicts. Oil shocks arrive quickly at Korean ports and even faster at Korean gas stations. The Bank of Korea has begun adjusting its tone. Yoo Sang-dai, the central bank’s senior deputy governor, recently suggested the time may have come not merely to pause rate cuts but to consider raising rates. That creates tension with the government’s expansionary fiscal instincts. President Lee Jae Myung has criticized what he calls fiscal conservatism and signaled support for active spending. Yet fiscal expansion becomes harder to sustain when inflation expectations are rising. Coordination between fiscal and monetary authorities will become increasingly important in the months ahead. The greater risk may lie in households rather than ministries. Margin lending in the stock market has climbed from 27 trillion won ($18.6 billion) at the end of last year to around 36 trillion won. In highly volatile markets, leverage often looks manageable until prices stop rising. The symbolism of the Kospi surpassing 7,000 points should not be dismissed. Korea’s capital markets have matured, and its chip industry now sits near the heart of the global AI economy. But stock indices are poor substitutes for economic balance. If the benefits of the export boom fail to spread beyond chipmakers and financial assets, the gap between market optimism and household reality will widen further. Markets, after all, can sprint ahead of economies for only so long before both rediscover the same altitude. editorial.title: [사설] 임성근 징역 3년 선고…이제 ‘VIP 격노’ 단죄 차례다
editorial.body: 2023년 7월19일 경북 예천군 수해 현장에서 무리한 수중 수색을 지시해 채 상병을 사망에 이르게 한 혐의로 구속기소된 임성근 전 해병대 1사단장에게 1심 재판부가 징역 3년을 선고했다. 업무상과실치사상 및 군형법상 명령 위반 등 주요 혐의 대부분이 유죄로 인정됐다.
editorial.pullQuote: 2023년 7월19일 경북 예천군 수해 현장에서 무리한 수중 수색을 지시해 채 상병을 사망에 이르게 한 혐의로 구속기소됐다.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Three-Year Prison Sentence for Lim Sung-geun... Now It's Time to Hold "VIP Rage" Accountable
summaryTr: Former Marine Corps 1st Division Commander Lim Sung-geun sentenced to three years in prison.
Soldier died following reckless underwater search orders at 2023 Yecheon flood disaster site.
Convicted of negligent death, violation of orders, and other major charges.
editorial.bodyTr: On July 19, 2023, a first-instance court sentenced former Marine Corps 1st Division Commander Lim Sung-geun to three years in prison. Lim had been indicted and detained on charges of directing reckless underwater search operations at a flood disaster site in Yecheon County, Gyeongbuk Province, which led to the death of Lance Corporal Chae. The court found him guilty on most of the major charges, including occupational negligence resulting in death and violation of orders under the Military Criminal Act.

editorial.titleTr: 50,000 BTS ARMY Gather Outside Mexico's Presidential Palace in Show of Cultural Soft Power
summaryTr: Fifty thousand BTS ARMY gathered in front of the Presidential Palace in Mexico City.
More crowds flocked there than at the Gwanghwamun concert in Seoul.
Korean Wave is expanding its cultural influence through fandoms across the globe.
editorial.bodyTr: On the 6th (local time), the Zócalo Plaza in front of the National Palace in Mexico City was packed with crowds. They were "ARMY" (BTS fan community) who came to catch a glimpse of the group BTS, who met with President Claudia Sheinbaum ahead of a local performance. The crowd that gathered that day numbered approximately 50,000, surpassing the attendance at BTS's first performance following their comeback at Gwanghwamun in Seoul. The ARMY members responded with thunderous cheers to BTS's Spanish greetings.

editorials.titleTr: Samsung Union Accepts Arbitration, Must Approach Negotiations with Constructive Stance
summaryTr: Samsung Electronics' management and labor have decided to accept mediation by the Central Labor Relations Commission.
As public concerns grew, they began renegotiations to resolve the dispute.
We urge them to achieve withdrawal of the general strike through rational decision-making.
editorials.bodyTr: Samsung Electronics' labor and management have agreed to accept the "post-dispute adjustment" proposed by the Central Labor Relations Commission. As concerns about the strike grew not only among politicians but also among the public, both sides have moved toward renegotiations to resolve the dispute. Of course, even with post-dispute adjustment, a withdrawal of the total strike cannot be guaranteed. Samsung Electronics' labor and management are urged to make reasonable decisions through dialogue that the public can also accept. The Samsung Electronics union said on the 8th that it will meet with management on the 11th and 12th...

editorials.titleTr: Road Worker Retirees' Rest Stop Racket: These Abuses Still Happening
summaryTr: A group of retired highway corporation workers has used rest areas as private pension reserves for 40 years.
The stark reality of monopolistic plunder of public assets under the guise of nonprofit organizations.
The blatant reality that corrupt practices of the former officials' cartel continue to run rampant.
editorials.bodyTr: The Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport's audit results on "Dosung-hoe," an organization of retired Korea Expressway Corporation employees, are nothing short of shocking. The group operated under the respectable guise of a nonprofit organization dedicated to "advancement of expressway construction technology," yet over the past 40 years it has undertaken no activities worthy of that mission. Instead, the audit revealed that the organization exploited public assets—rest areas throughout the nation's expressway system—as a private pension window for retirees. This exemplifies the revolving-door cartel between public institutions and their former officials.
